summary: Auth bug fix verified - JWT tokens now correctly stored in localStorage and sent via Authorization Bearer header. All protected endpoints work without 401 errors. Full auth flow tested: login -> dashboard -> settings -> new video -> logout.
passed_tests: POST /api/auth/login returns access_token and refresh_token in response body
POST /api/auth/register returns access_token and refresh_token in response body
POST /api/auth/refresh with Bearer refresh_token returns new access_token
GET /api/auth/me works with Bearer token
GET /api/auth/test-keys works with Bearer token (new endpoint)
GET /api/stats works with Bearer token
GET /api/projects works with Bearer token
GET /api/templates works with Bearer token
GET /api/settings works with Bearer token
GET /api/settings/api-keys works with Bearer token
GET /api/cost-logs works with Bearer token
POST /api/settings/api-key works with Bearer token
Protected endpoints return 401 without token
Full auth flow: login -> token stored -> subsequent pages load without 401
Frontend: Login stores tokens in localStorage
Frontend: Dashboard loads stats and projects (no 401)
Frontend: Settings page loads API keys, cost logs, providers (no 401)
Frontend: Test Keys button calls /api/auth/test-keys and shows results
Frontend: New Video wizard loads templates (no 401)
Frontend: Logout clears localStorage tokens and redirects to login
context_for_next_testing_agent: Auth bug fix is complete. JWT tokens are now stored in localStorage after login/register and sent via Authorization Bearer header for all API calls. The centralized axios instance in /app/frontend/src/lib/api.js handles token attachment via interceptors. All protected endpoints verified working. Test Keys button on Settings page calls GET /api/auth/test-keys endpoint.
rca_of_the_issue: The original auth bug was caused by relying on httpOnly cookies for cross-origin requests which is unreliable. The fix moved to localStorage + Authorization Bearer header pattern: 1) Backend returns tokens in response body, 2) Frontend stores in localStorage, 3) Centralized axios instance adds Bearer header to all requests, 4) Backend get_current_user() checks both cookies and Authorization header.
